docs.title: pymfx
docs.text: Python library for the Mission Flight Exchange (.mfx) v1.0 format.

docs.title: Installation
docs.text:
pip install pymfx
pip install pymfx[viz]        # folium + matplotlib
pip install pymfx[ds]         # pandas
pip install pymfx[notebooks]  # all extras + jupyter

docs.title: Features
docs.text: Feature API Parse / Write / Validate pymfx.parse() · pymfx.write() · pymfx.validate() Format conversion pymfx.convert.to_geojson/gpx/kml/csv() Flight statistics pymfx.flight_stats() Pandas DataFrame mfx.trajectory.to_dataframe() JSON serialisation mfx.to_dict() · mfx.to_json() Interactive maps viz.trajectory_map() · viz.speed_heatmap() · viz.compare_map() Matplotlib plots viz.flight_profile() · viz.events_timeline() · viz.flight_3d() Utilities generate_index() · merge() · diff() CLI pymfx flight.mfx --validate / --stats / --export / --diff

docs.title: Package structure
docs.text:
pymfx/
├── pymfx/
│   ├── __init__.py
│   ├── models.py
│   ├── parser.py
│   ├── writer.py
│   ├── validator.py
│   ├── checksum.py
│   ├── stats.py
│   ├── utils.py
│   ├── cli.py
│   ├── convert/
│   └── viz/
├── docs/
├── notebooks/
└── tests/

docs.title: License
docs.text: CC BY 4.0 — Format spec: mfx_spec_v1.0_final.md · MIME: application/x-mfx

docs.title: Changelog
docs.text: All notable changes to pymfx are documented here. Format follows Keep a Changelog.

docs.title: Added
docs.text:
• pymfx.parse() — read a .mfx file into Python objects
  • pymfx.write() — serialize a MfxFile to .mfx text (auto-computes checksums)
  • pymfx.validate() — full V01–V21 rule set from the .mfx v1.0 spec
  • pymfx.compute_checksum() / verify_checksum() — SHA-256 per spec §7
  • Dataclasses: MfxFile, Meta, Trajectory, TrajectoryPoint, Events, Event, Index, Extension, SchemaField
  • CLI: pymfx --validate, pymfx --checksum, pymfx --info
  • Notebooks: 01_quickstart.ipynb, 02_build_from_scratch.ipynb
  • Zero external runtime dependencies (Python ≥ 3.10 stdlib only)

docs.title: Setup
docs.text:
git clone https://github.com/jabahm/pymfx
cd pymfx
pip install -e ".[dev]"

docs.title: Run tests
docs.text:
pytest                        # run all tests
pytest --cov=pymfx            # with coverage

docs.title: Code style
docs.text:
ruff check pymfx/             # lint
ruff format pymfx/            # format

docs.title: Adding a converter
docs.text:
Create a new module under pymfx/converters/<format>.py with:

def from_<format>(source) -> MfxFile:
    ...

And expose it in pymfx/converters/__init__.py.

docs.title: pymfx.parse(source)
docs.text:
Parse a .mfx file and return a MfxFile object.

mfx = pymfx.parse("flight.mfx")      # file path (str or Path)

Raises ParseError on malformed input.

docs.title: pymfx.write(mfx, path)
docs.text:
Serialize a MfxFile to a .mfx file. SHA-256 checksums are computed automatically.

pymfx.write(mfx, "out.mfx")

docs.title: pymfx.validate(mfx)
docs.text:
Run all 21 validation rules (V01–V21) against a MfxFile.

result = pymfx.validate(mfx)
result.is_valid   # bool
result.errors     # list[ValidationIssue]
result.warnings   # list[ValidationIssue]

docs.title: pymfx.compute_checksum(lines)
docs.text:
Compute the SHA-256 checksum of a list of raw data lines, as defined in the .mfx spec §7.

checksum = pymfx.compute_checksum(raw_lines)

docs.title: Conversion — pymfx.convert
docs.text:
All functions accept a MfxFile and return a str.

Function Output format pymfx.convert.to_geojson(mfx) GeoJSON FeatureCollection pymfx.convert.to_gpx(mfx) GPX 1.1 pymfx.convert.to_kml(mfx) KML pymfx.convert.to_csv(mfx) CSV (one row per trajectory point)

docs.title: pymfx.flight_stats(mfx)
docs.text:
Compute aggregated flight metrics. Returns a FlightStats dataclass.

stats = pymfx.flight_stats(mfx)

FlightStats fields:

Field Type Description point_count int Number of trajectory points duration_s float Flight duration in seconds total_distance_m float Great-circle distance in metres total_distance_km float Property — total_distance_m / 1000 alt_max_m float Maximum altitude alt_min_m float Minimum altitude alt_mean_m float Mean altitude speed_max_ms float Maximum speed speed_mean_ms float Mean speed

docs.title: mfx.trajectory.to_dataframe(events=None)
docs.text:
Convert trajectory points to a pandas.DataFrame. Requires pandas.

df = mfx.trajectory.to_dataframe()
df = mfx.trajectory.to_dataframe(events=mfx.events)  # nearest-time merge

docs.title: mfx.to_dict()
docs.text: Recursively convert the entire MfxFile to a plain Python dict.

docs.title: mfx.to_json(indent=2)
docs.text: Serialize the MfxFile to a JSON string.

docs.title: Visualization — pymfx.viz
docs.text:
Requires pip install pymfx[viz].

Function Returns Description viz.trajectory_map(mfx) folium.Map Interactive GPS trace viz.speed_heatmap(mfx) folium.Map Segments coloured by speed viz.compare_map(flights, labels) folium.Map Multiple flights, one map viz.flight_profile(mfx) Figure Altitude / speed / heading over time viz.events_timeline(mfx) Figure Horizontal events timeline viz.flight_3d(mfx, color_by) Figure 3-D lon / lat / alt

All map and plot functions accept show_events=True/False.

docs.title: pymfx.generate_index(mfx)
docs.text:
Compute the [index] section from the trajectory and events data.

mfx.index = pymfx.generate_index(mfx)

Returns an Index(bbox, anomalies) where bbox = (lon_min, lat_min, lon_max, lat_max).

docs.title: pymfx.merge(mfx1, mfx2, gap_s=0.0)
docs.text:
Concatenate two flights in temporal order. The t values of mfx2 are shifted so the time axis stays strictly increasing.

combined = pymfx.merge(leg1, leg2, gap_s=5.0)

docs.title: pymfx.diff(mfx1, mfx2)
docs.text:
Structured comparison between two .mfx files. Returns a DiffResult.

result = pymfx.diff(mfx1, mfx2)
print(result)               # box-drawing table
result.has_differences      # bool
result.meta_diffs           # list of (field, val1, val2)

docs.title: MfxFile
docs.text: Field Type version str encoding str meta Meta trajectory Trajectory events Events \| None index Index \| None

docs.title: Meta — key fields
docs.text: id, drone_id, drone_type, pilot_id, date_start, date_end, duration_s, status, application, location, sensors, data_level, license, contact

docs.title: TrajectoryPoint
docs.text: t, lat, lon, alt_m, speed_ms, heading, roll, pitch (all optional except t, lat, lon)

docs.title: Event
docs.text: t, type, severity, detail

docs.title: Index
docs.text: bbox (tuple[float,float,float,float] | None), anomalies (int)

docs.title: Usage
docs.text: pymfx <file.mfx> [command] [options]

docs.title: --validate
docs.text:
Run all 21 validation rules. Exits with code 1 if errors are found.

pymfx flight.mfx --validate

✓ Valid file — no issues found.

docs.title: --checksum
docs.text:
Verify the SHA-256 checksums of [trajectory] and [events] sections.

pymfx flight.mfx --checksum

docs.title: --info
docs.text:
Print a human-readable summary of the file metadata.

pymfx flight.mfx --info

docs.title: --stats
docs.text:
Print aggregated flight statistics (duration, distance, altitude, speed).

pymfx flight.mfx --stats

┌─ Flight Statistics ────────────────┐
│ Points          :   3 600          │
│ Duration        :  360.0 s         │
│ Distance        : 1 245.3 m        │
│ Altitude max    :  102.3 m         │
│ Altitude min    :   48.1 m         │
│ Altitude mean   :   75.4 m         │
│ Speed max       :    8.5 m/s       │
│ Speed mean      :    6.2 m/s       │
└────────────────────────────────────┘

docs.title: --diff <file2.mfx>
docs.text:
Structured comparison between two .mfx files. Exits with code 1 if differences are found.

pymfx flight_a.mfx --diff flight_b.mfx

docs.title: --export <format> [-o <output>]
docs.text:
Export the trajectory to another format. Prints to stdout by default; use -o to write to a file.

pymfx flight.mfx --export geojson
pymfx flight.mfx --export gpx  -o flight.gpx
pymfx flight.mfx --export kml  -o flight.kml
pymfx flight.mfx --export csv  -o flight.csv

Supported formats: geojson, gpx, kml, csv.

docs.title: Parse, validate, write
docs.text:
import pymfx

mfx = pymfx.parse("flight.mfx")

print(mfx.meta.drone_id)
print(mfx.meta.date_start)
print(len(mfx.trajectory.points))

result = pymfx.validate(mfx)
if result.is_valid:
    print("✓ Valid")
else:
    for issue in result.issues:
        print(issue)

pymfx.write(mfx, "out.mfx")

docs.title: Convert
docs.text:
geojson = pymfx.convert.to_geojson(mfx)
gpx     = pymfx.convert.to_gpx(mfx)
kml     = pymfx.convert.to_kml(mfx)
csv     = pymfx.convert.to_csv(mfx)

docs.title: Flight statistics
docs.text:
stats = pymfx.flight_stats(mfx)
print(stats.duration_s)
print(stats.total_distance_km)
print(stats)   # formatted table

docs.title: Pandas DataFrame
docs.text:
# requires: pip install pymfx[ds]
df = mfx.trajectory.to_dataframe()
df = mfx.trajectory.to_dataframe(events=mfx.events)

docs.title: JSON serialisation
docs.text:
d  = mfx.to_dict()
js = mfx.to_json(indent=2)

docs.title: Visualization
docs.text:
# requires: pip install pymfx[viz]
import pymfx.viz as viz

viz.trajectory_map(mfx)
viz.speed_heatmap(mfx)
viz.compare_map([mfx1, mfx2], labels=["A", "B"])

viz.flight_profile(mfx)
viz.events_timeline(mfx)
viz.flight_3d(mfx, color_by="speed")

docs.title: Utilities
docs.text:
mfx.index = pymfx.generate_index(mfx)
combined  = pymfx.merge(leg1, leg2, gap_s=5.0)
result    = pymfx.diff(mfx1, mfx2)
print(result)
